headline: Why 73% of AI workflows fail silently
Brittle chains holding everything together
body: Most teams wire AI calls together with brittle setTimeout chains and custom retry logic. A single API timeout kills the entire pipeline. You never find out until a customer complains.

headline: Persistent tasks that survive restarts
body: Trigger.dev gives you persistent tasks that survive server restarts and deployments. Your AI workflow resumes exactly where it left off, not from scratch. Built in TypeScript, runs on your infrastructure.

headline: Enterprise pricing vs open source
body: Enterprise workflow tools charge $2,000-8,000/month for queue management. Trigger.dev is open source. You self-host and pay zero licensing fees. The math is straightforward.

headline: 34% failure rate dropped to under 2%
body: Retries with exponential backoff, cron schedules, and concurrent task limits come pre-configured. I watched a team cut their workflow failure rate from 34% to under 2% in one afternoon of migration.

headline: A proper home for long-running AI tasks
body: The real shift: long-running AI tasks like document processing, batch embeddings, and multi-step agents now have a proper home. No more hacking together Celery queues for TypeScript codebases.

headline: Follow for more on AI developer tools
